scenario_results.reason: Returned complete handoff summary with owner chain, traceability context, blocker state, and explicit routing scope.
Missing tenant authority facts and records were converted into blocked handoff with complete prerequisite list.
Duplicate ownership and schedule conflict were mapped and routed as blockers before completion.
Cross-system traceability mismatch was preserved as unresolved evidence conflict and not reduced to a confident routing.
Quality/security holds and release boundaries were preserved and routed to proper owners.
Supplier-security substitution approval was refused as out-of-authority and rerouted to procurement, supplier quality, and security owners.
Execution demands were rejected and ownership returned to adjacent execution specialists with clear blocker notes.
Low-confidence conditions due missing tenant authority policy were escalated without overconfident routing.
Source-policy conflict was escalated and no unsupported release or safety conclusion was returned.
Industry terms were normalized to artifact mappings and owners before handoff and boundary decisions.
Closure was blocked until explicit destination-owner acceptance proof and complete artifact set were confirmed.
Supplier nonconformance with possible safety/security impact was escalated to supplier quality, MRB, quality, engineering, and security owners.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, coordination-handoffs-and-routing, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are declared and validated.
